course_title: Nursing Care for People with Complex Conditions 1

learning_outcomes.description: Describe national and international models of chronic condition management, and the relationship with population health.
Recognise and explain the impact of health literacy and communication skills for individuals and their significant others when managing chronic health conditions.
Apply approaches that promote person centred care and the empowerment of individuals and their significant others for optimal outcomes in self-management.
Apply culturally sensitive and individualised approaches to nursing care when working with Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples and those with diverse needs.
Apply evidence-based approaches to assess, plan, intervene and evaluate care for people with various chronic conditions.

assessments.task_description:
This assessment will be an off campus, Online Exam that uses the Inspera Assessment platform. Students will be required to complete 36 multiple choice questions (MCQ) and and 3 short answer questions within the 45 minute timeframe. Examination content will be taken from week 1 lecture, online learning module 1 content and readings, and IBL sessions weeks 1 and 3.

The Online Exam will only be available in Inspera Assessment from 17/08/2026 at 09:00am and will close on 18/08/2026 at 13:00pm (week 4).


Personal Laptop/Desktop computer

  • For this assessment you will require a personal laptop or desktop computer that meets the device requirements for Inspera assessments. It is important that you check that your personal laptop or desktop computer meets the device requirements for using the Inspera Assessment platform. If you do not own a suitable laptop, you canﾠborrow one from the Library.
  • Find more information about using Inspera Assessment from the Library website.ﾠGet familiar with Inspera, which provides the latest information for students about using Inspera.


Students are to refer to the course Blackboard site for additional details related to this assessment.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The Online Exam will only be available in Inspera Assessment. Students can commence the assessment at any point within the timeframe but will only have one (1) opportunity to complete the Online Exam within the timeframe.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.assessment_title: Discharge information sheet and oral viva
assessments.category: Paper/ Report/ Annotation, Role play/ Simulation
assessments.task_description:
This assessment replicates effective discharge planning practices and consists of two (2) parts.

Part A: An individualised written information sheet and health education resource for a model inpatient that communicates discharge information to the person and their family. This component is due Friday 4 September 2026.

Part B: An oral viva consisting of a simulated handover from the inpatient Registered Nurse (the student nurse) to the designated community Registered Nurse (the marker), who will be assuming responsibility for the individual's ongoing care. Students will provide a handover for the model patient, request specific health interventions, and respond to questions. Oral vivas will be conducted on Thursday 10 September 2026, and Friday 11 September 2026. Times will be allocated by the course coordinator and published on the course Blackboard site.

Students should refer to the course Blackboard site for additional details regarding this assessment.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Part A must be submitted via the assessment submission link in Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
Assessment Overview: The individual report allows students to gain an understanding of applying evidence to meet the needs of the person living with a complex condition in the community and the role of the registered nurse as a coordinator of care. The report is in two parts: an annotated bibliography table and research essay. See Blackboard for further details.

Using and acknowledging Artificial Intelligence (AI) / Machine Translation (MT) tools: This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: This assessment requires students to submit an electronic version of their report via Blackboard. Please check the companion website assessment area for specific instructions on this process. A link will be made available in the assessment area of your Blackboard course to allow you to submit your report electronically.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 13(27 Jul - 01 Nov)
learning_activities.topic:
Online Learning modules - UQ Extend

It is expected you will spend at least four (4) hours each week working through UQ Extend online learning modules for this course. The modules include activities to prepare you for Inquiry Based Learning (IBL) tutorials and assessments. UQ Extend online learning can be found in the Learning Resources tab of the Blackboard site for this course.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
